title: DefinitionClassificationFrame
description: Schema for the rel.definition-classification frame: definitional and classification relations of the form 'X is a Y' or 'X is a type of Y'. This frame models a subject together with one or more supertypes, optional lemma-level modifiers, and an optional fallback gloss.

frame_type.description: Canonical frame type identifier for definitional / classification relations.

frame_family.description: Optional high-level family label retained for compatibility with older relational payloads.

subject.description: The entity being defined or classified (often the article's main subject).

definitional_relation.description: High-level semantic relation between subject and supertype(s). Recommended values include 'instance-of', 'subclass-of', 'role-of', and 'alias-of'.

supertype_entities.description: Entity-level supertypes that the subject belongs to, when the type itself is modeled as an entity (e.g. a named class, category, or organization-like type).

supertype_lemmas.description: Lemma-level labels for the type(s) that the subject belongs to, such as ['writer'], ['programming language'], or ['family', 'carnivorous mammal'].

modifier_lemmas.description: Lemma-level modifiers refining the classification, such as ['british'], ['fictional'], ['roman catholic'], or ['high-level', 'general-purpose'].

gloss.description: Optional short textual gloss or fallback definition snippet used only when structured classification data is incomplete.

attributes.description: Arbitrary structured attributes related to this definitional fact, such as source, source_property, confidence, or planning hints.

extra.description: Free-form metadata / provenance bag passed through unchanged, such as upstream statement IDs, raw extraction payloads, or debug notes.

EntityRef.description: Generic lightweight entity reference used across frame schemas.

id.description: Opaque internal or local identifier for the entity.

qid.description: External identifier such as a Wikidata QID.

name.description: Human-readable primary label for the entity.

label.description: Optional alternate display label; accepted for compatibility with upstream payloads that use 'label' instead of 'name'.

entity_type.description: Optional canonical entity type label, such as 'person', 'organization', 'class', 'language', or 'software'.

kind.description: Optional loose kind label retained for compatibility with older schemas and extracted payloads.

extra.description: Arbitrary metadata attached to the entity reference.
